text: Working with APIs is a common task for modern developers. First, you use HTTP requests to communicate with a server. Typically, you send a GET request to fetch data or a POST request to create new information. The server often responds with data in JSON format, which is easy to read and use in your code. Many APIs require authentication, so you must provide an API key or token to access their features. It is also important to respect rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a given time. Finally, you should handle errors gracefully by checking the status codes returned by the server, so your application can respond appropriately.
